h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: The facilities worker shifts the cart handle and asks whether the bracket is cleared for morning use; the tutor has to answer through the design question, not through reassurance.

“I might be sorting this wrong, but I think the stop has to show the bracket stops moving before the slip path reaches the hand-foot zone. Where I’m still stuck is the guard part: if the stop contains the twist but there’s still a reachable

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to say it doubles
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was proceeding with the assumption that doubling the side length simply doubles the area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: If the area only doubles, which two of these four do not count?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from abstract correction to a concrete visual constraint, forcing the learner to confront the physical impossibility of their 1D assumption on a 2D grid.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Test the same rule on a 3 cm square doubled to 6 cm: old area, new area, multiplier.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's newly formed 2D understanding by testing its generalizability with a new numerical example, moving from concrete to abstract.
